title: Use of eval() in Python
description: The use of eval() on untrusted input allows an attacker to execute arbitrary system commands or Python code, leading to a complete compromise of the application environment.
reasoning: eval() evaluates strings as Python expressions. When used with user-provided input, it creates an injection vulnerability.
suggested_fix: Use ast.literal_eval() to parse safely, or better yet, use json.loads() if the input is expected to be JSON.
